directRuntimePeerDependencies.purpose: Milestone release and dispute behavior depends on escrow payment setup and release semantics.

coreGuarantees: Gig acceptance verifies BOLA before lock or bid status checks.
Gig acceptance uses SETNX before OCC so only one bid can be accepted per gig.
Milestone contract immutable fields are compile-time constants, never tenant configuration.
Milestone release validates submitted delivery before funds movement.
Dispute handling never deletes milestone records and never moves funds on dispute creation.
Review records are append-only and review comments are excluded from PLATFORM_ONLY audit.

packageSigningConstraint.reason: FLOW-17 coordinates escrow, dispute, reputation, and bilateral review trust surfaces.

configurationKeys.description: Tenant-editable FLOW-17 marketplace operating policies. These values may vary by tenant without changing tenant isolation, escrow safety, review audit privacy, or event ordering.

keys.semantic: How long a gig acceptance lock remains active while bid acceptance completes.

boundedRange.rationale: Tenants may tune acceptance lock duration for marketplace pace, but BOLA, SETNX, OCC, and audit ordering stay fixed.

keys.semantic: Maximum number of milestones permitted in one freelancer contract.

boundedRange.rationale: Tenants may support simpler or more granular contracts while immutable field and sum guards remain fixed.

keys.semantic: Minimum milestone amount accepted by tenant marketplace policy.

boundedRange.rationale: Tenant pricing policy may vary while contract total validation and OCC writes remain MACHINE.

keys.semantic: Days after submitted delivery before eligible milestones may auto-release.

boundedRange.rationale: Tenants may tune auto-release timing, but delivery validation before funds movement remains fixed.

keys.semantic: Days a client has to dispute submitted milestone work before auto-release can proceed.

boundedRange.rationale: Tenants may adjust dispute policy without changing update-only dispute behavior or LIFO compensation.

keys.semantic: Maximum free-text length for bilateral marketplace reviews.

boundedRange.rationale: Tenants may tune review UX while audit records still exclude comment text.

keys.semantic: Days after contract close during which each party may submit one directional review.

boundedRange.rationale: Tenants may tune review timing while direction and duplicate guards remain fixed.

businessDomainAdaptation.description: Safe tenant adaptations are limited to marketplace operating policies and customer-facing module naming.

edgeCases.name: Enterprise procurement contracts
edgeCases.semantic: Tenant A allows more milestones and higher minimum milestone amounts for complex B2B work.
edgeCases.currentImplementation: T614 still rejects immutable field changes before storage and uses OCC for contract writes.

edgeCases.name: Fast turnaround marketplace
edgeCases.semantic: Tenant A shortens acceptance lock and auto-release windows for small repeat gigs.
edgeCases.currentImplementation: T613/T615 still keep BOLA, SETNX, OCC, delivery gate, and audit-before-event ordering.

edgeCases.name: High trust dispute policy
edgeCases.semantic: Tenant A extends the dispute window for higher-value marketplace work.
edgeCases.currentImplementation: T615 still uses update-only dispute state and no funds movement on dispute creation.

edgeCases.name: Portfolio rich reviews
edgeCases.semantic: Tenant A allows longer review comments after project close.
edgeCases.currentImplementation: T616 still enforces one review per direction and excludes comment text from PLATFORM_ONLY audit.

ironRulesMACHINE.description: Fixed invariants that tenant adaptation cannot change.
rules: Task IDs T613 through T616 remain fixed.
Gig acceptance BOLA verification must happen before lock acquisition.
Gig acceptance SETNX lock must happen before OCC bid status check.
GigAccepted is emitted only after audit storage succeeds.
Immutable contract field writes are rejected before any storage attempt.
Milestone sum validation must equal contract total before OCC write.
Delivery status must be SUBMITTED before any funds movement.
Dispute path uses update-only status changes and never deletes milestones.
Escrow compensation order remains REFUND_MILESTONE then RESTORE_GIG_STATUS.
Review directions remain CLIENT_TO_FREELANCER and FREELANCER_TO_CLIENT.
Review records remain append-only with duplicate direction rejection.
Review comment text is never written to PLATFORM_ONLY audit records.
All state events preserve store-before-enqueue ordering.

tenantAEnterpriseMarketplace.description: Tenant A tunes the marketplace for enterprise service contracts.
tenantAEnterpriseMarketplace.impact: Contracts can be longer and review/dispute windows are more conservative while acceptance, escrow, and audit safety remain unchanged.
tenantAEnterpriseMarketplace.testable: Fork CI and live auth checks still pass after tenant.config.json and freedom-config.defaults.json record these values.

notPartOfAdaptationSurface.description: MACHINE elements that must not change during tenant adaptation.

passCriteria: Tenant adaptations may change only declared FLOW-17 configuration values or display/module metadata.
All four services remain MicroserviceBase subclasses and use TenantContextResolver for tenant scope.
Auth checks still return 401 for missing or cross-tenant token and 403 for wrong-role token after adaptation.
Forked package CI must pass npm install, TypeScript, Jest, and provenance steps.
